summary: Matched the common fighter EscapeAir code by adding `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_EscapeAir.c` and its header, removing the corresponding hand-written assembly from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`, wiring the new C object into `obj_files.mk`, and adding the motion-variable/common-data fields needed to express air-dodge behavior. The PR report shows the new file and all listed functions at 100.00% match.

key_files.role: New matched C implementation for EscapeAir functions.
New declarations for `ftCo_80099A58`, `ftCo_80099A9C`, EscapeAir callbacks, and the landing callback helper.
Removed the matched EscapeAir assembly block and updated remaining assembly callsites from local label `.L_80099A58` to symbol `ftCo_80099A58`.
Added `mv.co.escapeair` motion vars with `timer` and saved `Vec3 self_vel`; also changed several named nested structs in the union to anonymous structs.
Typed ftCommonData offsets used by EscapeAir as `escapeair_deadzone`, `escapeair_force`, `escapeair_decay`, and adjacent floats/ints.
Tightened prototypes for related helpers from `HSD_GObj*` to `ftCo_GObj*` so EscapeAir can call them cleanly.

what_changed: `ftCo_80099A58` checks shoulder-button input with `fp->input.x668 & (HSD_PAD_R | HSD_PAD_L)`, starts EscapeAir through `ftCo_80099A9C(gobj, p_ftCommonData->x334)`, and returns a bool.

`ftCo_80099A9C` saves current self velocity into `fp->mv.co.escapeair.self_vel`, computes EscapeAir launch velocity from stick direction using `ftCommon_8007D9D4`, `cosf`, and `sinf`, or zeros velocity if stick input is within `escapeair_deadzone`.

EscapeAir state setup clears `cmd_vars[cmd_skip_decay]`, stores the timer in `fp->mv.co.escapeair.timer`, changes motion state to `ftCo_MS_EscapeAir`, calls `ftAnim_8006EBA4`, and sets common state via `ftCommon_8007EBAC(fp, 23, 0)`.
`ftCo_EscapeAir_Anim` transitions out through `ft_80096900` when animation frames are exhausted, using `p_ftCommonData->x340` and `p_ftCommonData->x344`.

`ftCo_EscapeAir_IASA` handles the timer window, calls `ft_80095328`, optionally restores saved self velocity, decrements the timer after `ftCo_800D705C`, and applies a `0.1f` horizontal velocity multiplier when `ftCo_800C3B10` succeeds.
`ftCo_EscapeAir_Phys` either decays x/y self velocity by `p_ftCommonData->escapeair_decay` or delegates to `ft_80084DB0` depending on `cmd_vars[cmd_skip_decay]`.
`ftCo_EscapeAir_Coll` now calls `ft_80082C74(gobj, ftCo_80099D70)`, and `ftCo_80099D70` calls `ft_800D5CB0(gobj, 0, p_ftCommonData->x344)`.
Removed sdata2 constants for `0.0f`, `1.0f`, and `0.1f` from the remaining assembly block because the matched C file now owns those constants.

Changed multiple assembly branch-and-link references in `ft_08A4.s` from `.L_80099A58` to the named C symbol `ftCo_80099A58` for callsites in pass, bury jump, passive wall, jump, aerial jump, parasol fall, rebirth wait, and capture jump related code.
smart_moves: Introduced a small local enum `cmd_var_idx` with `cmd_skip_decay` instead of using a raw command-var index throughout the new file.
Used a static inline helper (`inlineA0`) to isolate the EscapeAir velocity setup while preserving the matched call/stack behavior of `ftCo_80099A9C`.

Typed previously unknown common-data fields only where the new decomp needed evidence: `escapeair_deadzone` as `Vec2`, `escapeair_force` and `escapeair_decay` as floats, while leaving less-certain neighbors as generic names like `x334`, `x340`, and `x344`.
Added explicit motion-variable storage for EscapeAir at fp+2340/fp+2344, matching the assembly pattern where a timer and saved velocity are stored and later reused.
Kept `MUST_MATCH` guarded unused stack arrays where necessary, documenting them with `@todo Unused stack` instead of hiding matching artifacts.
Renamed external assembly callsites to the new C symbol immediately, which makes future slices easier to search and avoids dangling local-label references after partial asm removal.
decomp_lessons: When moving a block out of a large assembly file, update all remaining assembly callsites to the new exported C symbol, not the old local label.
For common action-state code, model fp+2340 motion variables in `ftCommon_MotionVars` as a named union member once the state-specific meaning is clear.
Prefer evidence-backed field names for common-data offsets that are directly used by the matched code, but avoid overnaming adjacent fields whose semantics are not proven.
Small `MUST_MATCH` stack-padding arrays can be acceptable when the function otherwise cleanly expresses behavior and the padding is clearly marked as an unused-stack matching artifact.

Use existing project typedefs (`ftCo_GObj`, `Fighter_GObj`, `Vec2`, `Vec3`) to make function prototypes and struct fields reflect observed usage rather than leaving everything as `HSD_GObj*` or `UNK_T`.
Common fighter state transitions often require both motion-state change and auxiliary calls (`ftAnim_8006EBA4`, `ftCommon_8007EBAC`); preserving call order is part of matching.
naming_conventions: New file and public callbacks follow the common-action naming style: `ftCo_EscapeAir.c`, `ftCo_EscapeAir.h`, `ftCo_EscapeAir_Anim`, `ftCo_EscapeAir_IASA`, `ftCo_EscapeAir_Phys`, and `ftCo_EscapeAir_Coll`.
Unidentified but address-stable functions are kept as address names such as `ftCo_80099A58`, `ftCo_80099A9C`, and `ftCo_80099D70`.
New semantic common-data names use action prefixes: `escapeair_deadzone`, `escapeair_force`, and `escapeair_decay`.
Motion vars are accessed as `fp->mv.co.escapeair.timer` and `fp->mv.co.escapeair.self_vel`, matching the ftCommon union member naming pattern.
Header declarations include address comments like `/* 099A58 */`, consistent with the project’s function-address annotation style.
The header guard uses the address-style guard `GALE01_099A58`, matching nearby generated/decomp headers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed 254 lines from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` corresponding to the matched EscapeAir functions while leaving unrelated assembly in place.
Added `ftCo_EscapeAir.c.o` to `TEXT_O_FILES` before the residual `ft_08A4.s.o`, allowing the new C object to replace only the matched text range.
Used `ABS(fp->input.lstick.x)` and `ABS(fp->input.lstick.y)` deadzone checks to reproduce the assembly’s compare/fneg behavior around stick magnitude.
Used `cosf` and `sinf` from `<MSL/trigf.h>` to reproduce the velocity projection in the original assembly.
Represented `0.1f` directly in C for the IASA horizontal velocity dampening, corresponding to the removed `.L_804D8688` sdata2 constant.
Used guarded dummy stack bytes in `ftCo_80099A9C`, `ftCo_EscapeAir_Anim`, and `ftCo_EscapeAir_IASA` to satisfy stack-layout requirements under `MUST_MATCH`.
The PR's generated report lists the file and each function at `0` score / `100.00%`, indicating a complete match for the submitted C file.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type `p_ftCommonData->x334`, `x340`, or `x344` after EscapeAir.
Search for other uses of `cmd_vars[cmd_skip_decay]` or command variable index 0 in EscapeAir animation command handling.
Look for later cleanup removing the `MUST_MATCH` unused stack arrays in `ftCo_EscapeAir.c`.
Search for PRs that continue decompiling the remaining functions in `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` around `ft_80099D9C`.
